page: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance characteristics, particularly in single-core and text-processing workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2207.0 and a multi-core score of 18364.0, placing its single-core, image processing, and text processing capabilities in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded operations are supported by advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which facilitate accelerated matrix and vector operations. This is reflected in strong bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speeds of 227.98 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU score of 51718.7.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, the c8i.8xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads rather than memory-heavy databases or high-capacity storage applications. It is highly efficient for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 56.69 GB/sec, and Redis operations, processing 59.81 million SET operations per second. Additionally, its AMX support enables efficient low-latency CPU-based machine learning tasks, demonstrating a top-tier text generation speed of 357.67 tokens/second for small LLM models.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. It excels in compute-heavy tasks, showing top-tier performance in single-core benchmarks, image processing, and text processing. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, it is highly efficient for CPU-based machine learning inference, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression. The c8i.8xlarge is best suited for high-performance computing, web serving, and compute-intensive application servers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU-based processing and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 64 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for static web serving, Redis operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
